atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +1,27 @@; async registry crate manifest plus root workspace member/dependency wiring
atoms.source_snippet:
[package]
name = "ritual-async-registry"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/ritual-net/ritual-reth-internal"
description = "AsyncJobTracker contract state reading for Ritual Network"

# ARTIFACT 1 VERIFICATION: Dependencies chosen to avoid cycles
# - NO ritual-* dependencies (prevents cycles)
# - Minimal external dependencies (matches ritual-async-inspector pattern)

[dependencies]
# Primitives (from Artifact 3 - needed for Address, Bytes, B256, U256)
alloy-primitives.workspace = true

# Database trait (from Artifact 2 - Database<DB> generic bound)
revm.workspace = true

# Error handling (from Artifact 3)
thiserror.workspace = true

# Logging (from Artifact 3)
tracing.workspace = true

[dev-dependencies]

# root Cargo.toml wiring:
"crates/ritual-async-registry/"
ritual-async-registry = { path = "crates/ritual-async-registry" }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence introduces `ritual-async-registry` as a standalone AsyncJobTracker state-reader crate with minimal shared dependencies and no `ritual-*` crate dependencies, preventing dependency cycles. The root workspace must also include the crate as a workspace member and workspace dependency so downstream crates can reference it by workspace path.

destinations.evidence: The source diff adds a 27-line new manifest at diff lines 42565-42597. The destination manifest preserves the same package metadata, dependency comments, four workspace dependencies, empty `[dev-dependencies]` section, and absence of `ritual-*` dependencies.

The destination root `Cargo.toml` preserves `crates/ritual-async-registry/` in `workspace.members` and `ritual-async-registry = { path = "crates/ritual-async-registry" }` in `[workspace.dependencies]`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination keeps the manifest text and root workspace wiring required by the source divergence: same crate identity, same minimal dependency set, same workspace membership, and same workspace dependency path. That preserves the async registry crate's build graph role exactly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing the root workspace dependency or adding a `ritual-*` dependency to the registry manifest would either break workspace path resolution for users of the crate or reintroduce the dependency cycle the source manifest explicitly avoids.
atoms.notes: This atom covers only the async registry manifest and root workspace wiring, not any `crates/ritual-async-registry/src/*` implementation files.

summary: The async registry crate manifest and root workspace wiring are exact-port preserved: package metadata, minimal workspace dependency set, explicit cycle-avoidance dependency shape, workspace member entry, and workspace dependency path all match the source divergence.
notes: Queue entry verified as line 290: A	crates/ritual-async-registry/Cargo.toml.
The source file diff starts at line 42565 and the Cargo.toml hunk ends at line 42597 before the next `crates/ritual-async-registry/src/error.rs` header at line 42598.
Destination `crates/ritual-async-registry/Cargo.toml` contains the same 27-line manifest body as the source-added file.
Destination root `Cargo.toml` includes `crates/ritual-async-registry/` in workspace members and `ritual-async-registry = { path = "crates/ritual-async-registry" }` in workspace dependencies.
No review queue entry was required because the audited manifest and workspace wiring are preserved exactly.
